source.title: Sensory-specific predictive models in the human anterior insula

confidence_rationale: Synthesis commentary comparing two independent cross-modal pain-expectancy studies (Sharvit 2018, Fazeli & Buchel 2018). Argues explicitly that the anterior insula runs sensory-specific predictive models, with analogous but dissociable machinery for pain, disgust, and other aversive modalities. Lower weight than empirical entries because it is a commentary/review, but valuable for the architectural synthesis it provides.
failure_signatures: A single cross-modal aversive predictive model is falsified by sensory-specificity of the anterior insula PE.
Does not directly test within-pain stream separation.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The anterior insula maintains multiple sensory-specific prospective models, one per aversive modality (pain, disgust), rather than a single general unpleasantness predictor. Sub-portions of the middle-anterior insula differentiate expectancy signals from prediction-error signals.
mapping.ree_translation: Sensory-specific prospective models in the same anatomical region is the biological precedent for REE's per-stream forward-model family (MECH-256/257 -- single substrate with gated readout). By extension, within pain, it is coherent to have one shared substrate that exposes different heads for z_harm_s and z_harm_a. The paper supports option (a).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The studies reviewed distinguish pain from disgust, not A-delta from C-fiber within pain. The within-pain extrapolation is by analogy.
mapping.source_context: Review/commentary paper synthesising two empirical fMRI studies.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality lower because it is a review/commentary rather than primary empirical data. Mapping fidelity high because it directly frames the architectural question.
